title: polygala kurdica subsp. kurdica
intro: Polygala kurdica subsp. kurdica, also known as Kurdish milkwort, is a plant species within the polygala family, specifically a subspecies of Polygala kurdica, native to the Middle East. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Polygala kurdica subsp. kurdica include its potential to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion or tincture, with the flowers, leaves, or roots being used separately or in combination to create a medicinal tea or other preparations.
